data.name: AltPhrase
data.value: Let's dive deep - ready to handle the pressure of technologically entrenched environments

data.name: TextCompumate
data.value: Rapidly got promoted from just helping ERP customers to QA department and backlog analysis. Right after that I was designing, coding, testing, publishing and maintaining that same software. In addition to developing, I'm reference on SQL troubleshooting, Windows and IT infrastructure. My top responsabilities are API integrations, social communication services, AI and our website.

data.name: IntroductionA
data.value: I'm coding C# everywhere. From creating custom controls and type libraries in legacy frameworks, to now reimagining them on the newest web based platforms.

data.name: IntroductionB
data.value: I've designed several services and ported them to Visual Basic winforms through COM interoperability, many involving advanced things like threads, non-native asynchronous code, and typed events. Also, I maintain a commercial softwarehouse website in ASP NET with MVC architecture and reusable Razor page components. Recently I built multiple minimal APIs from scratch, with features like authentication, dynamic routing, and built-in Swagger documentation.

data.name: IntroductionC
data.value: Today, most of my work is focused on developing with Blazor, while also leveraging knowledge in mobile projects using .NET MAUI Blazor Hybrid. I'm also building our AI ecossystem with open-source tools and MCP.

data.name: MainPhrase
data.value: Ready to launch — excited to explore the unknown and help shape the future of technology

data.name: TextPMVA
data.value: Started teaching colleagues to deal with IT equipments, productivity tools and public sector ERP. For the first time I coded a real page in a website using HTML, CSS and JavaScript and published it. I also helped coordenate in core processes like biddings, approvals and requirements elicitation. This was key to understand corporate relationships, team collaboration and end user perspective.

data.name: InfoCompumate
data.value: Full Stack Developer, QA Analyst, 2020 - today

data.name: RefCompumate
data.value: Right hand of Supreme Master of Universe

data.name: Myself
data.value:
I'm a pragmatic .NET developer — but above all, a person deeply bounded to computers. It was not even a choice; At 7 years old, already on this century, I had my first interaction with one: an Amiga, where I spent hundreds of hours playing Chuckie Egg. From that moment on, I began having countless experiences with digital technology throughout my childhood.
Not long after that, I got an already-old Win95, where I started to manage things like Paint, Office, Printers, Local Network, and other tools — all on my own. That happened because I was in a particularly challenging environment: I knew no one who could help me, the dial-up Internet only worked after midnight, unreachable for a boy still at primary school, with no computer classes for years to come.
Those difficulties, followed by that (coffee) unique feeling of victory after fixing problems, shaped me into the professional I am today — fearless.

data.name: Philosophy
data.value: "In the midst of the debate between OOP and functional programming, I embrace challenge-oriented programming"
